Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Text Ruling

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication.

(see word minutes for full details)

Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order

DEFENDANT ROBERT JOHNSON's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Order signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel John Mlnarik and The Mlnarik Law Group, Inc., counsel of record for Defendant Robert A. Johnson. The motion is OFF CALENDAR.

On August 11, 2025, the court issued a tentative ruling denying counsel's earlier infirm motion to be relieved as counsel. Further, the court ordered counsel to "lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 8/12/2025 a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim." The next day, the court adopted its tentative ruling. A month has passed, and the court has yet to receive counsel's proposed order. Counsel may renew this motion after he lodges the proposed order and serves the executed order on all parties entitled to service. Counsel should note that motions to be relieved as counsel are generally not granted if the trial date is near.

This is an order of the court. Violations of written court orders may be addressed through sanctions, up to and including contempt.

Counsel must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 9/9/2025 a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim.

No proposed order received. Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2025 Set For Trial On Jan-05-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Johnson 415-714-3063 Defendant.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. The Court announces there was timely notice of contest received and the court usually does not hear matters which are not contested. Mr. Johnson addresses the court and understands he is not in Pro Per yet. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The Mlnarik Law Group, Atty. John L. Mlnarik's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Robert A. Johnson (Robert A. Anderson).

Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel John Mlnarik and The Mlnarik Law Group, Inc., counsel of record for Defendant Robert A. Johnson. The motion is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Moving Parties did not timely file proper proof of service of the moving papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) It also appears the motion was not timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b); Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail to place within State adds five court days to "any period of notice"].) Finally, the motion is addressed to "Robert A. Anderson," and no such person is a party in this case.

Moving Parties must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 8/12/2025 a proposed order quoting the above text verbatim.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
